key_insight: A conditional denoising diffusion model can forecast brittle crack growth in phase-field fracture while preserving temporal coherence and providing localized uncertainty estimates.
engineering_relevance: This offers a faster surrogate to high-fidelity fracture FEM for long-horizon crack evolution prediction, with uncertainty information useful for damage monitoring and design decisions.
method: Conditional DDPM trained on rolling damage histories
Uses phase-field velocity and gradient magnitude as kinematic proxies
Autoregressive re-sampling enables stochastic multi-step rollout and uncertainty quantification
evidence_signal: Across held-out regimes, the model achieved 0.12 px mean crack-tip error, Dice = 0.929 ± 0.010 over 50-step rollout, and high-uncertainty pixels identified high-error predictions with 90% precision.
watch_item: Inference is still relatively expensive versus deterministic surrogates and performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the fracture training data.
paper_title: Uncertainty-Aware Crack Growth Forecasting via Conditional Denoising Diffusion Models for Phase-Field Fracture
